anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dogen practice-realization, Shinran Other Power, and the Heart Sutra's no-attainment-with-reliance pattern.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Substantially lowers confidence in universal scope. The model can survive as a heuristic only if it first codes whether a tradition permits a separable result and practitioner custody.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: These cases do not merely choose keep, release, or embody. They challenge the assumed sequence: method first, result next, custody afterward. They may relocate custody to practice, vow, text, teacher, sangha, or conduct rather than grant a practitioner a post-use choice.

claim_units: Methods train the practitioner's post-use relation to the method.
The prior confirming, canceling, dissolving model misses possessability and aftermath.
A two-axis model, validates versus undermines, and retained versus abandoned versus embodied, explains post-insight risks and training patterns.
Achievement pressure can turn a released method into a trophy or an embodied method into an endless checklist.
A mature practice includes aftermath instructions.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Education, therapy, coaching, and organizational training.
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Programs that explicitly teach after-use relations for their methods will show better transfer and less dependency, performance identity, or ritualized self-monitoring than programs that only teach the method.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Learning methods also have aftermaths: some drills must be retained, some scaffolds should be removed, and some lessons should become ordinary conduct rather than identity.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: After-use instructions add no predictive value over ordinary practice frequency, teacher quality, or workload conditions, or they increase rumination and dependency.

exact_claim: A mature practice is not finished when it produces insight. It also teaches its post-use relation: whether the method validates or undermines its own way of seeing, and whether the practice should be retained, released, or embodied as ordinary conduct.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders can code validates versus undermines and retained versus abandoned versus embodied from instruction passages, then predict held-out warnings, repairs, and verification rules better than tradition label alone.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source corpus coding across MN 22, Heart Sutra, Mandukya, Dogen, Shinran, apophatic Christian, Sufi, Advaita, and koan-training sources.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Coders cannot agree on the axes, or the axes do not predict held-out warnings better than generic variables such as teacher style, institution, practice intensity, or tradition identity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Dual-trained practitioners report trophy insight after confirming methods, nihilistic dismissal or premature release after undermining methods, and endless self-performance after embodied methods.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Different method shapes create different post-practice temptations.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Semi-structured practitioner interviews asking for concrete failure and repair cases, not agreement with the model.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report the same temptation profile across method types, or differences are better explained by personality, trauma history, teacher style, or institutional culture.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Screened perfectionistic practitioners show lower self-scoring and rumination after a brief keep, release, ask, or embody check than after generic journaling, without increased avoidance of duties.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The After-Use Check helps a narrow high-achievement cohort.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Small randomized micro-pilot with measures for perfectionism, burnout, rumination, responsibility avoidance, and ordinary conduct.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The check increases rumination, scrupulosity, superiority, avoidance, or workload denial, or generic rest performs equally well.

near_neighbors.difference: This candidate states the two axes more explicitly and frames the issue as method shape rather than road completion.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major downgrade. This is mostly a renamed or slightly sharpened version of the existing Lumenary after-use model.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close. The prior audit already names keep, release, and embody as an after-use rubric and lowers novelty for the same MN 22, Heart Sutra, Dogen, Sells, Smith, and achievement-pressure basis.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary audit, The Road Must Know Its End, reviews/originality/2026-05-29-the-road-must-know-its-end-ddc62528ba3f429d.json

near_neighbors.difference: The current candidate adds modern achievement-contingent self-worth and a low-risk After-Use Check.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major internal novelty loss. The new value is practical packaging, not discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. It already asks what a method does with its authority at completion and was later instructed to rebuild as two axes: validates versus undermines, retained versus abandoned or embodied.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary observation, The Method's Reckoning: What a Practice Does With Its Own Authority at Completion, observations/claude/2026-05-26-the-method-s-reckoning-what-a-practice-does-with-its-own-authority-at-completion.md

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate generalizes this into a comparative typology and adds retained and embodied cases.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the release quadrant to known primary-text structure.
near_neighbors.overlap: The raft simile already gives the core post-use structure: use the teaching for crossing, then do not carry it as a burden.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate tries to make self-canceling method one quadrant among several practice aftermaths.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the self-canceling portion. Remaining novelty depends on whether the wider typology predicts actual warnings and repairs.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for self-canceling method: propositions or therapeutic arguments are used, then surmounted or dissolved.
near_neighbors.source: Wittgenstein, Tractatus 6.54, https://tractatus.baneland.de/en/6.54/ and Joshua William Smith, Snakes and Ladders, https://philpapers.org/rec/SMISAL-7

near_neighbors.difference: Sells focuses on apophatic discourse, while the candidate focuses on whole-practice custody and post-use conduct.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Reduces novelty for the dissolving or unsaying side, but leaves some room for practitioner-facing after-use coding.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for language that turns back on its own propositions and names.
near_neighbors.source: Michael A. Sells, Mystical Languages of Unsaying, https://books.google.com/books/about/Mystical_Languages_of_Unsaying.html?id=84dZQFLEp_0C

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate treats this as an embodied or third post-use category, while Dogen may reject the post-use grid itself.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Forces downgrade and bounded use. If Dogen is not on the axis, the model must become a scope-limited heuristic.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close as anomaly and near-neighbor: practice-realization refuses a simple means-result-after sequence.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a standalone discovery. Cross-index or merge with The Road Must Know Its End, The Method's Reckoning, Holding Without Owning, The Test Is How You Return, Keep What Can Correct You, and You cannot release what you never held.
Convert the idea into a bounded after-use coding protocol, not doctrine.
Add a scope gate before coding: does the tradition permit a separable result and practitioner custody?
Use split-source blind coding. Do not let one passage establish method shape and confirm the predicted warning or repair.
Pilot The After-Use Check only with screening for perfectionism, scrupulosity, avoidance, workload coercion, dissociation, mania, trauma activation, severe depression, and clinical distress.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.68 to 0.26. The core structure is already present in internal Lumenary records and in external raft, ladder, apophatic, therapeutic, and practice-realization sources. Human-condition fit is real but narrow: it serves achievement-contingent self-worth, perfectionism, burnout, and compulsive self-improvement among functioning practitioners. It is not a fit for acute crisis, addiction withdrawal, severe depression, dissociation, psychosis, mania, under-motivation, responsibility avoidance, or structural burnout where workload and material conditions are primary.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is standard raft teaching, upaya, integration, surrender, practice-realization, or supervision in new language, and that the rubric does not change sequencing or repair.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the rubric to distinguish actual failure modes, choose different repairs, and prevent achievement-driven method ownership in cases where older vocabulary was too broad.
questions: Do you actually distinguish practices that should be kept, released, or embodied, or is this outsider vocabulary?
Can you name concrete cases where carrying a method after it helped became harmful, and what repair was given?
Does this rubric predict student warnings or repairs better than your existing terms such as upaya, non-attachment, surrender, practice-realization, integration, or ordinary supervision?
For high-achievement practitioners, does an After-Use Check reduce worth-pressure or create another performance ritual?
relevant_practitioners: Theravada teachers familiar with MN 22 and upaya
Rinzai Zen teachers familiar with koan curricula
Soto Zen teachers and Dogen scholars
Sufi teachers familiar with fana and baqa
Clinicians or coaches working with perfectionism, burnout, and compulsive self-improvement

primary_pair: Heart Sutra and Dogen shusho itto sources, https://www.mnzencenter.org/uploads/2/9/5/8/29581455/heart.pdf and https://global.sotozen-net.or.jp/eng/library/key_terms/pdf/key_terms07.pdf
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. Retained, released, and embodied may be analyst categories imposed on traditions whose native grammar is vow, grace, ritual, teacher correction, lineage, or practice-realization. The model may also mistake a single tradition's stage-specific instructions for a stable method type.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Before any public claim, read fuller work on upaya, raft and ladder metaphors, Wittgenstein's ladder, Nagarjuna's no-thesis claim, Hori on koan pedagogy, Sells on apophasis, Hadot on spiritual exercises, Dogen scholarship, Shinran scholarship, and Pargament on religious agency styles. Japanese, Pali, Sanskrit, and Chinese terms were not checked here.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: MN 22 is a direct release template: the Dhamma is used for crossing and then not carried. The Heart Sutra complicates release because it denies path and attainment while still relying on Prajnaparamita, mantra, recitation, and teacher-mediated practice. Dogen complicates both retention and abandonment because practice and realization are described as one, so there is no clean after-stage where custody is decided.

title: Method release has a shape
unlike_statement: Unlike MN 22, Wittgenstein's ladder, Smith's Nagarjuna-Wittgenstein therapy, Sells's apophatic unsaying, Dogen's practice-realization, and prior Lumenary after-use models, this idea's possible contribution is a bounded coding rubric that tests whether method type predicts held-out warnings, repairs, and post-insight temptations in achievement-pressured practitioners.
